error.signature: AI tells someone in Peru to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Peru does not use 911. Dial 105 for police, 116 for fire (Bomberos), and 106 for ambulance (SAMU). All are free from any phone.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Peru expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Peru. Police is 105, fire is 116, and ambulance is 106. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.why_fails: 105 is the national police line; ambulances are dispatched via 106 (SAMU). Use the right number to avoid delay.

workarounds.action: For medical emergencies dial 106 (SAMU); 116 for fire, 105 for police.
workarounds.how: 106 ambulance, 105 police, 116 fire; note private clinics also run ambulances in Lima.
